sub_sections.title: Objectives and ambition
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO targets call HORIZON-CL4-2026-05-DIGITAL-EMERGING-02, advancing next-generation AI agent architectures from TRL 2 at project start to TRL 5 by project end across all primary demonstrator contexts. The project pursues eight measurable objectives. OBJ-1 (Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition) achieves ≥40% improvement in plan success rate over pure LLM-based baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on WebArena, SWE-bench, and GAIA for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps. OBJ-2 (Unified adaptive memory architecture) achieves ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in interaction sequences exceeding 50 agent steps. OBJ-3 (Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols) achieves effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents with ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent agent baselines and formal proof of protocol convergence. OBJ-8 (External Tool and API Orchestration Layer) achieves a typed tool registry supporting ≥30 tools/APIs, tool invocation success rate ≥95%, mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s, and full provenance traces for 100% of invocations. OBJ-4 (Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support) achieves diagnostic reasoning accuracy within 10% of specialist clinician performance on 500 de-identified cases across 5 diagnostic domains at NIHS. OBJ-5 (Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation) achieves ≥15% reduction in defect rates and ≥10% reduction in energy consumption at ISIA's pilot factory over a 3-month operational period. OBJ-6 (Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation) achieves ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time and ≥15% improvement in delivery schedule adherence across 3 ELI supply chain corridors. OBJ-7 (Open framework release and ecosystem contribution) achieves open-source release with ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of release, contributions accepted to the AI-on-demand platform, results validated through ≥2 TEFs, and technology transfer initiated through ≥3 EDIHs. MAESTRO surpasses the state of the art by integrating three currently disconnected research communities — LLM-based agents, classical planning and multi-agent systems, and memory-augmented neural networks — into a single unified co-designed architecture that provides formal guarantees over long-horizon tasks that pure LLM chain-of-thought approaches cannot offer.

sub_sections.title: Methodology
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's methodology is structured around three interdependent research pillars unified by a cross-cutting External Tool and API Orchestration Layer and validated through three sector demonstrators co-designed with industry partners in operational environments. Pillar 1 (WP2, led by ATU) combines Monte Carlo Tree Search and hierarchical task networks with LLM-driven heuristic evaluation, providing sub-task graph decomposition with formally specified pre-conditions, post-conditions, and verification checkpoints; plan monitoring enables agent self-correction from verified intermediate states, directly overcoming LLM fragility over extended task horizons. Pillar 2 (WP3, led by BIIS) develops a tri-store memory architecture — working memory, episodic memory, and semantic memory — managed by a learned memory controller that governs information flow between stores, enabling coherent long-horizon agent behaviour beyond context-window limits. Pillar 3 (WP4, led by CERIA) develops formally specified task-delegation and negotiation protocols with provable convergence guarantees and auditable interaction traces that human operators can inspect. The External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (WP2, led by ATU) provides a typed tool registry, formal tool-selection policies integrated with the planning engine, standardised API adapters for REST, GraphQL, HL7 FHIR, and OPC-UA protocols, post-invocation verification and grounding, execution monitoring with fallback/recovery, and auditable traces stored in adaptive memory — addressing SR-04 as an architectural requirement rather than a bolt-on interface. WP8 (led by FIIT) integrates all three pillars into a unified system and produces a community benchmark suite (D8-02, MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset, month 42). The project's interdisciplinary approach bridges formal methods, machine learning, clinical informatics, and industrial engineering, with each demonstrator WP co-led by a domain-specialist partner (NIHS for healthcare, ISIA for manufacturing, ELI for logistics). MAESTRO is primarily technical in character; SSH integration is not required for this AI architecture research agenda. The gender dimension in R&I content is not directly applicable to core AI architecture development; diversity principles will be applied to benchmark dataset curation, including demographic coverage of clinical cases in WP5 validation. Open science practices are central to the methodology: all frameworks, models, and benchmarks are published open-source (D9-02, Open-source software release, month 36, led by BAL) and shared via the AI-on-demand platform; a Data Management Plan (D1-02, due month 6, led by ATU) will ensure FAIR compliance for all project datasets.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: OBJ-1: ≥40% plan success rate improvement over LLM baselines on WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA
OBJ-2: ≥30% task coherence/factual consistency improvement at >50 agent steps
OBJ-3: ≥5 agents coordinated, ≥25% joint task completion gain, formal convergence proof
OBJ-4: diagnostic accuracy within 10% of specialists on 500 cases across 5 domains at NIHS
OBJ-5: ≥15% defect reduction, ≥10% energy reduction at ISIA pilot factory over 3 months
OBJ-6: ≥20% disruption recovery improvement, ≥15% schedule adherence across 3 ELI corridors
OBJ-7: open-source release ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months, AI-on-demand, ≥2 TEFs, ≥3 EDIHs
OBJ-8: ≥30 tools/APIs, ≥95% invocation success, ≤5s recovery, 100% provenance traces
TRL 2 at project start progressing to TRL 5 by project end across primary demonstrators
Three disconnected research communities (LLM agents, classical planning, memory-augmented) as SotA gap MAESTRO bridges
Partner short names, legal names, and WP lead assignments (ATU, BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, BAL, FIIT)
Call scope requirements SR-01 to SR-04 (LLM agents, planning, memory, external tools) confirmed covered
API adapter protocols REST, GraphQL, HL7 FHIR, OPC-UA in External Tool and API Orchestration Layer
D1-02 Data management plan due month 6 (ATU); D9-02 Open-source software release month 36 (BAL)
D8-02 MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset month 42 led by FIIT (WP8 Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking)
